text:
$AAOI looks a bit extended but no denying the strength both in the photonics basket as well as this name specifically. 200 should come in short order if this holds up.

Two ways to play it: retest of the 10d SMA for the lower-risk entry, or a breakout through 160.

Watch LITE, GLW and the rest of the photonics basket for confirmation, always best to see names within a group/theme move together.

text: @aleabitoreddit $VGO.WA Vigo Photonics is my favourite long on photonics sector.

Yes, that's the photonics chain powering next-gen AI interconnects. Sivers' DFB lasers pair with POET's Optical Interposer for scalable light engines in CPO and pluggable transceivers. POET enables Celestial AI's Photonic Fabric platform, which Marvell acquired to supercharge optical scale-up for their AI chips and data centers. Clear tailwind for $SIVE in this ecosystem.

text:
Frontrunning 1.6T/CPO within the broader photonics supercycle is the most compelling investment to me. 

I have high conviction in that statement.

Which is why I'm long the entire supply chain (+1 extra bottlenecK)

1. $SIVE - Their laser revenue scales aggressively with $JBL, $MRVL, Ayar, O-Net.

And I do think CPO/1.6T will blow away any conservative analyst projections from how hard $NVDA, $GOOGL, and others have been pushing photonics architectures.

Downside risk is multi-sourcing, but there's a reason Jabil chose Sivers. When you compare $MTSI, $LITE, $COHR, Furukawa, and others.

There's genuinely not many laser suppliers in the entire world... they're all $10B+, then you have this mini CHIPS act chokepoint trading at <$1B MC.

2. Shunsin (6451) - I don't see how it's possible Foxconn's optical foundry for testing, packaging, and assembly is valued at $1.5B MC less than $LWLG. 

When they look extremely derisked piggybacking off of Foxconn's photonics volume.

$TSM's optical arm VisEra example is ~$5B, but they scale H2 2028 from Gen-3. Foxconn looks to be ramping up just next year.

They're just scaling low fwd p/e multiples off of $NVDA CPO supply chain demand in Taiwan and all public indicators point to capacity expansion + extreme demand. 
 
3. Win Semi - They're the foundry for Sivers to scale up DFB laser production. As well as $AVGO, SpaceX supply chains and others.

When I do supply chain mapping and Win Semi pops up in every single frontier supply chain I see. 

There's probably something markets are not pricing in.

4. $MRVL - I find this genuinely compelling as a mini-Broadcomm. 

Their potential design with with $GOOGL today, helps the case past 2028.

But the catalyst I was looking at was $MSFT Maia ramp, which happens H2 2026, and likely keep scaling up exponentially into 2027, 2028, 2029. 

Celestial acquisition was probably the smartest thing in the world for them. Maybe on next drop or CSP? 

5. $HPS.A - Transformers/Switchgears are commodities  + boring parts of the DC supply chain.

However, when the bottleneck is 2-5 years, and you have backlog increasing 100%+... causing extreme shortages.

It's only up 20%+ since my thesis post, but I do see this being de-risked given massive backlog visibility (even though it's inferred, they don't give exact #). 

I do think markets are missing something, especially with potential gross margin expansion from price hikes if they pull it off.... 

Again backlog + demand just de-risks this company,  and it seems like a high growth compounder post facility expansion last year. 

There's many others like $NBIS, $JBL, $RPI, $TSEM, $LITE, $ARM, $SOI, $AXTI,  $IQE, $ALRIB, Fittech, PCL, and others that I'm very fond of, but just mentioning 5 off the top of my head from today's prices... if I'm creating a new portfolio. 

Of course, it's good to barbell with other uncorrelated companies to AI supply chains, but these are just 5 I liked.

"Frontrunning 1.6T/CPO within the broader photonics supercycle is the most compelling investment to me. I have high conviction in that statement. Which is why I'm long the entire supply chain (+1 extra bottlenecK) 1. $SIVE - Their laser revenue scales aggressively with $JBL, $MRVL, Ayar, O-Net. And I do think CPO/1.6T will blow away any conservative analyst projections from how hard $NVDA, $GOOGL, and others have been pushing photonics architectures. Downside risk is multi-sourcing, but there's a reason Jabil chose Sivers. When you compare $MTSI, $LITE, $COHR, Furukawa, and others. There's genuinely not many laser suppliers in the entire world... they're all $10B+, then you have this mini CHIPS act chokepoint trading at <$1B MC. 2. Shunsin (6451) - I don't see how it's possible Foxconn's optical foundry for testing, packaging, and assembly is valued at $1.5B MC less than $LWLG. When they look extremely derisked piggybacking off of Foxconn's photonics volume. $TSM's optical arm VisEra example is ~$5B, but they scale H2 2028 from Gen-3. Foxconn looks to be ramping up just next year. They're just scaling low fwd p/e multiples off of $NVDA CPO supply chain demand in Taiwan and all public indicators point to capacity expansion + extreme demand. 3. Win Semi - They're the foundry for Sivers to scale up DFB laser production. As well as $AVGO, SpaceX supply chains and others. When I do supply chain mapping and Win Semi pops up in every single frontier supply chain I see. There's probably something markets are not pricing in. 4. $MRVL - I find this genuinely compelling as a mini-Broadcomm. Their potential design with with $GOOGL today, helps the case past 2028. But the catalyst I was looking at was $MSFT Maia ramp, which happens H2 2026, and likely keep scaling up exponentially into 2027, 2028, 2029. Celestial acquisition was probably the smartest thing in the world for them. Maybe on next drop or CSP? 5. $HPS.A - Transformers/Switchgears are commodities + boring parts of the DC supply chain. However, when the bottleneck is 2-5 years, and you have backlog increasing 100%+... causing extreme shortages. It's only up 20%+ since my thesis post, but I do see this being de-risked given massive backlog visibility (even though it's inferred, they don't give exact #). I do think markets are missing something, especially with potential gross margin expansion from price hikes if they pull it off.... Again backlog + demand just de-risks this company, and it seems like a high growth compounder post facility expansion last year. There's many others like $NBIS, $JBL, $RPI, $TSEM, $LITE, $ARM, $SOI, $AXTI, $IQE, $ALRIB, Fittech, PCL, and others that I'm very fond of, but just mentioning 5 off the top of my head from today's prices... if I'm creating a new portfolio. Of course, it's good to barbell with other uncorrelated companies to AI supply chains, but these are just 5 I liked."}}}, "quoted_status_result": {"result": {"__typename": "Tweet", "core": As AI clusters scale past copper’s physical limits, the bottleneck shifts to optical & these are the companies building that layer across the stack: 

1. $AAOI building the transceiver layer of the AI network through vertically integrated U.S.-based InP laser manufacturing. It has already secured over $200M in its first volume 1.6T order from one hyperscale customer followed by another $124M in 800G orders from a second.

2. $AEHR building the reliability layer for the optical & AI hardware stack through burn-in & test systems. It just received a record $41M follow-on order from its lead hyperscale customer reinforcing the idea that Sonoma is becoming a key production burn-in platform for high-power AI ASICs.

3. $CRDO building the connectivity layer that helps AI clusters move data faster through active electrical cables, retimers & high-speed interconnect silicon. The DustPhotonics acquisition also extends that platform into silicon photonics before copper becomes a real constraint.

4. $LITE building the laser layer of the AI optical stack through EMLs, optical components & optical switching exposure. The setup is backed by a $2B $NVDA strategic investment & optical circuit switch backlog above $400M with orders reportedly extending through 2028.

5. $VIAV building the testing & validation layer of the optical stack through network instrumentation & photonics measurement tools. It is the picks-and-shovels layer of the transition because every high-speed optical buildout still needs to be tested regardless of which transceiver vendor wins.

6. $COHR building one of the core photonics bottlenecks through indium phosphide lasers, optical engines & communications components tied to next-gen AI networking. It also has a $2B $NVDA strategic investment behind it & is doubling InP device capacity into the 1.6T ramp.

7. $MRVL building the DSP & optical infrastructure layer through electro-optics, PAM DSPs, interconnect silicon & custom networking chips. The Celestial AI deal & NVLink Fusion exposure both strengthen its position as photonics becomes more central to AI cluster design."}}}, "rest_id": "2045885512922419251", "source": "<a href="https://mobile.twitter.com" rel="nofollow">Twitter Web App</a>", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "408684", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2046003571603423359", "source": "<a href="https://mobile.twitter.com" rel="nofollow">Twitter Web App</a>", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "10975", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
This map shows why Sivers - ( $SIVE ) is quietly becoming one of the most important names in AI photonics.

Sivers sits in the center of the CPO ecosystem supplying the critical multi-wavelength laser arrays to:

•  Jabil (1.6T LRO pluggables)
•  Ayar Labs (optical I/O)
•  O-Net + Enablence (external light source modules)
•  POET (hybrid integration, now tied to Marvell + Google TPU discussions)

That’s direct or indirect exposure to $META , $AMZN , $MSFT , $GOOGL , and $MRVL — basically the entire hyperscaler map.

Still only ~$720M market cap

The Nasdaq listing is coming. The commercial ramps are coming.

Marvell $MRVL Announced they will be acquiring Polariton Technologies 

They plan to add high speed low power plasmonics based silicon photonics devices to its portfolio

This gives Marvell a stronger presence in optical technology and positions them better for next gen connectivity

text:
$poet and $sivef indirect benefits to this $mrvl acquisition-

$poet Technologies (POET) sees indirect positive momentum, not a direct tie to Polariton itself.POET’s core tech is its Optical Interposer platform—a wafer-scale, passive-alignment hybrid photonics solution that integrates light sources, modulators, waveguides, etc., into compact optical engines/light engines. It’s upstream in the same AI interconnect value chain Marvell is aggressively building out.
Marvell’s prior Celestial AI acquisition (Dec 2025) already made POET a beneficiary: Celestial used POET’s tech (e.g., Blazar light sources) as a key enabler for its Photonic Fabric. Marvell inherited that supply relationship, and POET has confirmed purchase orders/shipments tied to it.

This new Polariton deal further validates Marvell’s heavy commitment to scaling photonics for AI (now adding plasmonic modulation on top of SiPh). It doesn’t displace POET—it reinforces the ecosystem demand for integrated optical engines. Analysts and investors have repeatedly called out POET as a “real winner” or potential M&A target in the wake of Marvell’s optics moves, as hyperscalers/NVIDIA/Marvell need proven, scalable integration platforms. 

$POET gets another leg of validation and potential order acceleration. It’s not a direct “Polariton synergy” play, but the Marvell optics flywheel helps POET’s positioning in the same AI connectivity wave.

 $sivef is a strong indirect beneficiary and one of the more credible pure-plays in the exact layer this deal targets.Sivers Photonics specializes in high-performance DFB laser arrays, CW (continuous-wave) lasers, and external light source modules explicitly designed for AI data-center optical interconnects, CPO, pluggables, and SiPh integration.
They have established partnerships in the ecosystem (e.g., with POET for light engines, Ayar Labs, and paths into Marvell/Celestial/NVIDIA optics roadmaps via the CW-WDM MSA and related collaborations). Their lasers are a critical “fuel” for SiPh-based modulators and engines—like the plasmonic + SiPh solutions Marvell just acquired.
This deal accelerates adoption of exactly the architectures Sivers supplies (1.6T+ LRO transceivers, CPO, ultra-low-power AI interconnects). Recent Sivers news (e.g., Jabil collaboration on 1.6T modules) already shows momentum; Marvell’s move pours more fuel on the fire for laser array demand.

text:
$GFS GlobalFoundries 

定位:AI 数据中心 → 光互联 → 硅光(Silicon Photonics)代工厂

坐标系:
光引擎里的两种主流技术路线:一种基于磷化铟(InP,做激光最好,也是 Sivers / AXT 的主场),另一种基于硅(硅光 / SiPh,做调制和波导最适合 CMOS 量产)。

硅光的思路是"把光学器件用成熟半导体工艺做在硅晶圆上",好处是能借用半导体行业几十年积累的产能、良率、成本曲线。问题是硅本身不会发光,所以硅光要和 InP 激光配合使用 —— 这是 POET 这类"封装平台"存在的原因。

硅光需要专门的工艺和专门的晶圆厂。GlobalFoundries 就是这个赛道的最大代工厂。

公司背景:
美国 Malta, NY 总部,全球纯代工厂第三名(TSMC、Samsung 之后,和 UMC、SMIC 同级)。不做最先进制程(3nm/5nm 是 TSMC 的地盘),专做 12–28nm 的特殊工艺节点 —— 射频、模拟混合信号、功率管理、SOI、以及硅光。

2025 年 11 月收购了新加坡 AMF(Advanced Micro Foundry),这是全球第一家商业化硅光纯代工厂,拥有 200mm 硅光平台。

加上之前收购的埃及 InfiniLink(光学数据连接芯片创业公司),GlobalFoundries 宣称自己是全球最大的纯代工硅光晶圆厂。

技术路线:
纽约工厂做 300mm 硅光,新加坡(AMF)做 200mm 硅光,两地互补。

GF 同时在做 InP 激光和硅 CMOS 的单片异构集成,长期目标是做出"一块晶圆上同时有激光、调制器、驱动、波导"的完全集成光引擎。

赛道位置:
硅光代工这个环节格局是:Intel 做自用的(自己的 CPO 产品)、TSMC 给 Broadcom 做、GlobalFoundries 是最大的纯代工(向所有人开放)。

Broadcom、Marvell、Cisco、Coherent、Lumentum 这些公司的硅光芯片,相当一部分会在 GF 的晶圆厂生产。

它的地位相当于硅光赛道的"TSMC" —— 不做自己品牌产品,做所有人的代工。

财务和公司状态:
市值约 200 亿美金,2026 年硅光业务预计贡献 AMF 收购后 +7500 万美金,目标 2030 年前硅光年收入突破 10 亿美金。

硅光市场本身以 25% CAGR 增长。

主体业务(非硅光)还是传统代工,受汽车和工业需求拖累,财务表现平淡。2026 年 3 月做了二次公开发行 + 并行股份回购,市场反应一般。

所以 GFS 的股价走势更像"被硅光故事带动的大盘股",不是纯粹硅光标的。

AI 周期里的角色:
"硅光代工"的稀缺标的。

想直接投硅光,美股里几乎没有纯正选项(Ayar Labs 未上市,Lightmatter 未上市),GFS 是公开市场最直接的硅光赋能者 —— 但要意识到它的非硅光业务占比还很大,硅光故事被稀释。

核心赌注:
硅光在 2027–2030 年真正从 CPO 需求起量;
GF 在开放代工市场能拿住大部分份额;
主体代工业务不继续恶化。

仓位定位:
硅光主题的代理持仓(proxy play),不是纯正选项。

和 POET / Sivers 形成互补:POET / Sivers 是"如果硅光/CPO 跑通 10 倍股"的押注,GFS 是"硅光跑通它至少有稳定订单"的底仓。

适合追求硅光敞口但又不想承担 early-stage 波动的配置。

关键观察点:
AMF 整合进度和新加坡硅光产能爬坡 → 
Broadcom / Marvell 等大客户的硅光订单走向 → 
主体代工业务汽车/工业板块能否止跌。

硅光的卖铲人。

{"richtext_tags": []}, "text": "$GFS GlobalFoundries 定位:AI 数据中心 → 光互联 → 硅光(Silicon Photonics)代工厂 坐标系: 光引擎里的两种主流技术路线:一种基于磷化铟(InP,做激光最好,也是 Sivers / AXT 的主场),另一种基于硅(硅光 / SiPh,做调制和波导最适合 CMOS 量产)。 硅光的思路是"把光学器件用成熟半导体工艺做在硅晶圆上",好处是能借用半导体行业几十年积累的产能、良率、成本曲线。问题是硅本身不会发光,所以硅光要和 InP 激光配合使用 —— 这是 POET 这类"封装平台"存在的原因。 硅光需要专门的工艺和专门的晶圆厂。GlobalFoundries 就是这个赛道的最大代工厂。 公司背景: 美国 Malta, NY 总部,全球纯代工厂第三名(TSMC、Samsung 之后,和 UMC、SMIC 同级)。不做最先进制程(3nm/5nm 是 TSMC 的地盘),专做 12–28nm 的特殊工艺节点 —— 射频、模拟混合信号、功率管理、SOI、以及硅光。 2025 年 11 月收购了新加坡 AMF(Advanced Micro Foundry),这是全球第一家商业化硅光纯代工厂,拥有 200mm 硅光平台。 加上之前收购的埃及 InfiniLink(光学数据连接芯片创业公司),GlobalFoundries 宣称自己是全球最大的纯代工硅光晶圆厂。 技术路线: 纽约工厂做 300mm 硅光,新加坡(AMF)做 200mm 硅光,两地互补。 GF 同时在做 InP 激光和硅 CMOS 的单片异构集成,长期目标是做出"一块晶圆上同时有激光、调制器、驱动、波导"的完全集成光引擎。 赛道位置: 硅光代工这个环节格局是:Intel 做自用的(自己的 CPO 产品)、TSMC 给 Broadcom 做、GlobalFoundries 是最大的纯代工(向所有人开放)。 Broadcom、Marvell、Cisco、Coherent、Lumentum 这些公司的硅光芯片,相当一部分会在 GF 的晶圆厂生产。 它的地位相当于硅光赛道的"TSMC" —— 不做自己品牌产品,做所有人的代工。 财务和公司状态: 市值约 200 亿美金,2026 年硅光业务预计贡献 AMF 收购后 +7500 万美金,目标 2030 年前硅光年收入突破 10 亿美金。 硅光市场本身以 25% CAGR 增长。 主体业务(非硅光)还是传统代工,受汽车和工业需求拖累,财务表现平淡。2026 年 3 月做了二次公开发行 + 并行股份回购,市场反应一般。 所以 GFS 的股价走势更像"被硅光故事带动的大盘股",不是纯粹硅光标的。 AI 周期里的角色: "硅光代工"的稀缺标的。 想直接投硅光,美股里几乎没有纯正选项(Ayar Labs 未上市,Lightmatter 未上市),GFS 是公开市场最直接的硅光赋能者 —— 但要意识到它的非硅光业务占比还很大,硅光故事被稀释。 核心赌注: 硅光在 2027–2030 年真正从 CPO 需求起量; GF 在开放代工市场能拿住大部分份额; 主体代工业务不继续恶化。 仓位定位: 硅光主题的代理持仓(proxy play),不是纯正选项。 和 POET / Sivers 形成互补:POET / Sivers 是"如果硅光/CPO 跑通 10 倍股"的押注,GFS 是"硅光跑通它至少有稳定订单"的底仓。 适合追求硅光敞口但又不想承担 early-stage 波动的配置。 关键观察点: AMF 整合进度和新加坡硅光产能爬坡 → Broadcom / Marvell 等大客户的硅光订单走向 → 主体代工业务汽车/工业板块能否止跌。 硅光的卖铲人。"}}}, "rest_id": "2047074469135655314", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "122", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text: TXN showing signs that Analog Chip Season may finally be taking off. I’ve been talking about this coming transition in the hardware trade since Q4 of last year and it finally seems like it may be upon us. The first screenshot below hurts my soul, me pumping Photonics in early December but not longing any of them, but that’s the macro tourist game for ya. Regardless, this signals the continuing evolution of the manufacturing boom going on globally. In a time where energy constraints are picking up, any hardware that can bypass those constraints will get bid up here big time, and that is exactly what Analog chips provide, energy efficiency. Listen I know I’ve been banging the drum on potential trouble coming to the AI Datacenter Buildout but I don’t think it is going to break yet or crash the economy yet. As long as NVDA remains elevated, even if flat, capital from that sector will continue to slosh around into different ancillary corners of the same trade. I.E. as NVDA has languished since Q3 of last year, memory stocks have been on a tear. I think we are going to see now a rotation from this existing capital base start going into the Analog semiconductor names. I think as manufacturing output begins to diversify away from AI, I think having exposure to Analog Semis will help buttress any pain from the AI Hardware trade that should come, once demand destruction starts getting actualized. I think I’m going to finally get some exposure to them as I think it is a great asymmetric hedge to my Downstream AI thesis being expressed thru META RDDT & IBM calls.

text:
Asian supply chain leaks just revealed the Holy Grail of wearables, and the market is completely missing the underlying silicon photonics chokepoint. 🚨 

Let’s connect the dots on Point 6 regarding the Apple Watch Ultra and the non-invasive blood glucose sensor. If you want to know who is building the light engine, look at the attached slide from $SIVE / $SIVEF. 🧵👇

1️⃣ The Leak: Miniaturized Lasers for Health
According to the supply chain leak below, Apple is targeting April next year for its blood glucose feature. The tech? Non-invasive spectroscopy using miniaturized lasers to collect reflected wavelengths through the skin.

2️⃣ The F100 Client Connection
Look at the attached slide from Sivers Semiconductors ($SIVE). A mystery "US Fortune 100" client has invested ~$18M in NRE to develop custom Indium Phosphide (InP) optical sensors for "Consumer devices" (notice the smartwatch icon). Apple is Top 5 on the Fortune 100.

3️⃣ The Timeline Matches Perfectly
Sivers delivered an order of 50,000 chips in Q1 2024 to this F100 client. 50k is not mass production—it is the exact volume required for EVT/DVT (Engineering & Design Validation Testing) prototype runs. A prototype run in early 2024 aligns perfectly with an aggressive commercial launch in Spring 2027.

4️⃣ The True TAM (Total Addressable Market)
The slide explicitly states that when this project enters the volume phase, the Request For Proposal (RFP) ranges from $30M to several $100M per year.

While retail investors are looking at historical P/E ratios of a legacy telecom business, they are completely blind to the fact that $SIVE has spent years building the miniaturized InP laser engine for the biggest consumer health revolution of the decade.
The data is right there for anyone willing to do the math. 1+1=2. 🍏⌚️

⚠️ Disclaimer: Supply chain leaks are unverified rumors by nature and timelines can shift. However, investing is about connecting high-probability data points. The technological and chronological overlap here is simply too precise to ignore. As always, DYOR.

text:
I’m now long $POET

$POET is one of the wildest turnaround stories in photonics, not so long ago I was bearish with the hard dilution around this company. 

Rewind to early 2024. Company was near insolvent. Nearly ran out of cash. Forced into an emergency raise. Share count has nearly 4x’d in two years.
Fast forward to today:
+$ 313M cash on the balance sheet (vs ~$1.3B market cap)
+ Just raised $150M in January at $7.25 from institutional investors
+ 152M shares out vs 40M-ish pre-2024
+ Marvell orders now confirmed by the CFO
+ Foxconn and Luxshare partnerships already disclosed
+ Strategic tie-up with $SIVE on integrated light sources

The dilution was brutal. But look at what it bought: a wafer-level optical interposer platform that Marvell, Foxconn, Luxshare, and Sivers are all plugged into.

The old POET was a story stock with $41K in revenue. The new POET just 25x’d revenue YoY and is embedded in the CPO supply chain right next to $SIVE

A raising phoenix as it seems.

I’m long since yesterday. Great continuation today up 20% and still a long way to re rate.

Ooo the irony...You point this out and you immediately get two Swedish jante guys chasing you...

Since the spending time on the wrong things and don't see the photonics ecosystem revaluation..they don't get it..

This picture show a "peer" and customer to Sivers..Just look at the valuation..Sivers looks cheep in this comparison. What the don't get is this revaluation is about something else, not at all about SIVE in it self..it is about the ecosystem below AI, hyperscalers, Nvidia/ AMD and so on...I told them all many years
 ago that this will happen. But the same thing then but but EBIT is bad...EBIT is bad still due to write off capitalized R&D and M&A...mostly none CF items. 
God speed! 🍺

Proof-of-concept prototypes → hybrid systems → software stack → wafer-scale production.

Shkreli just positioned himself as the gateway to Artificial General Intelligence and Artificial Super Intelligence. 🚀

The photonics report that began our initiation of the sector. 

Leading to our equity reports on $SIVE and $POET so far, with many more to come. 

Report hits on $LITE quite often.

It gives you three clear discovery lenses, CPO‑dominant clusters, OCS‑flattened fabrics, and RF‑photonics edge/SatCom.

Then, links each to specific public and private “probe” names.

Extremely in depth, report is located below.

CPO‑dominant clusters( $NVDA/ $AVGO/ $TSMC/ $LITE, $MRVL ‑Celestial), 

Optical circuit‑switched fabrics (LITE OCS, Google/Meta hyperscalers) that break switch‑radix limits.

text:
Light Speed: Why Photonics Is the Invisible Backbone of AI

KEY HIGHLIGHT

- The Marvell $MRVL + Inphi angle is the one most people underestimate. DSP chips are to optical transceivers what the engine is to a car — you cannot ship a high-speed optical module without one, and Marvell owns that market position. Every 800G and 1.6T transceiver deployed in an AI cluster is a Marvell DSP royalty.

- Coherent $COHR is the most comprehensive pure-play but also the most complex story — the II-VI/Finisar merger created integration challenges and a leveraged balance sheet. The thesis is right but execution risk is real. Worth sizing accordingly.

- Fabrinet $FN is the sleeper that rarely gets discussed. They are the contract manufacturer for most of the photonics industry, which means they get volume leverage without technology concentration risk. When the whole industry is growing, Fabrinet grows with all of it.

- Credo $CRDO is the highest-beta, most speculative name on the list — but also the one with the most potential to surprise to the upside if the AI networking upgrade cycle accelerates faster than expected.

- And the private company to watch most closely is Ayar Labs — if optical chiplets become the standard interface for next-gen AI processors, whoever owns that technology gets acquired at a price that will seem obvious in retrospect.

DSP chips are to optical transceivers what the engine is to a car — you cannot ship a high-speed optical module without one, and Marvell owns that market position. Every 800G and 1.6T transceiver deployed in an AI cluster is a Marvell DSP royalty. - Coherent $COHR is the most comprehensive pure-play but also the most complex story — the II-VI/Finisar merger created integration challenges and a leveraged balance sheet. The thesis is right but execution risk is real. Worth sizing accordingly. - Fabrinet $FN is the sleeper that rarely gets discussed. They are the contract manufacturer for most of the photonics industry, which means they get volume leverage without technology concentration risk. When the whole industry is growing, Fabrinet grows with all of it. - Credo $CRDO is the highest-beta, most speculative name on the list — but also the one with the most potential to surprise to the upside if the AI networking upgrade cycle accelerates faster than expected. - And the private company to watch most closely is Ayar Labs — if optical chiplets become the standard interface for next-gen AI processors, whoever owns that technology gets acquired at a price that will seem obvious in retrospect. text:
A lot of people are saying “invest in photonics, quantum, deep tech”

Reality is most retail including me don’t actually have an edge there

Unless you’re building this stuff or working in it every day, you’re mostly guessing

Like I can look at $COHR $LITE $POET and read about them

But do I really know what separates one from the other at a deep level?

And more importantly would I know when one quietly falls behind? Probably not

Same thing with chips

I see $NVDA $AMD $ASML $TSM all winning from the data center buildout
But if something shifts and one starts losing would I catch it early? Unlikely

That’s why I own $SMH than pretend I can out analyze engineers

Your edge isn’t chasing the most technical thing

Your edge is understanding what you actually use and see every day

I use $META daily. Everyone around me uses it. I can feel if engagement is going up or down

That’s real signal

There’s always going to be a “next bottleneck” or the next shiny theme

Most people chasing it are just rotating narratives not actually investing with an edge

Stay within what you understand my friends

text:
@bdinvestingg The “photonics kills copper” take misses the point.
Photonics moves data. Copper delivers power.
With ~200GW of data centres in backlog, that’s 5–8M tonnes of copper demand waiting.
AI isn’t reducing copper—it’s increasing it.

$MXL Recap (2/2)

ℹ️ Setup: Gap & Go 

Undiscovered name that's supplying the photonics boom, 140% this week!

➡️Saw constant buying pressure in premarket
➡️Joined in the trend early > scaled into the 50 psych breakout 
➡️Held into open  as we got a push towards 60 (no 60 fill on am move🫠)
➡️Clouds held and great higher low price action into 10am so I scaled in more as PA got tight
➡️Got the move to HOD > 60 breakout!
➡️Locked into strength over 60
➡️wanted 65 on this but gave up midday
➡️Stopped out when 5/12 clouds broke

text:
Copper is dead. AI data centers need light — and photonics companies can’t build fast enough to keep up.

$LITE – Makes the lasers inside every optical transceiver. No LITE, no light.
$AAOI – Builds transceivers that convert electrical signals to optical. The translator.
$MRVL – Designs the silicon brains that control photonic data flow at speed.
$GLW – Invented optical fiber. Still makes the glass the internet runs through.

text:
Three distinct approaches to quantum hardware currently dominate the sector's market activity: cloud-accessed trapped ions, quantum annealing with hybrid solvers, and room-temperature integrated photonics.

The industry is not converging on a single architecture. It is branching out, with different players betting on fundamentally unique paths to commercial value. This diversity proves the field is in its experimental prime, where the ultimate hardware winner remains undecided and may never narrow to just one modality.

Yet the true common thread is accessibility. Through cloud delivery, advanced developer frameworks, and intuitive circuit creation platforms, the race has permanently evolved. It is no longer just a raw contest of qubit counts. The defining metric is how quickly platforms can put usable quantum capability into the hands of enterprise teams.

The commercial layer of quantum computing is forming in real time. The organizations building robust bridges between complex physics and practical business problems are the ones shaping what comes next #QuantumComputing

text:
什么是硅光子（Silicon Photonics）
最简单的理解：
传统芯片用电子传输信息，硅光子用**光子（光）**传输信息。

传统方式：电信号 → 铜线传输 → 慢、热、耗能
硅光子：光信号 → 光纤传输 → 快、冷、省电

硅光子在AI里的具体应用
Co-Packaged Optics（CPO，共封装光学）：
这是最重要的趋势，把光模块直接封装到交换机芯片旁边：

传统方式：
交换机芯片 → 电信号 → 插拔式光模块 → 光纤

CPO方式：
交换机芯片 + 硅光子模块（合为一体）→ 光纤

text:
The AI infrastructure bottleneck nobody talks about.

As AI clusters scale past copper's physical limits, optical photonics becomes the critical layer.

These companies are building the infrastructure layer that powers the next generation of AI systems—and the investment signals that come with them.

A massive data throughput upgrade is coming. The question is which companies capture the value.

text:
I've been quietly watching on the sideline for a couple of months now.

Everybody and their grandmas are hyping photonics (and for a very good reason, no doubt on that!) but there are truly a few people doing the actual work and a ton of parrots.

A handful of persons truly do the research and others just copy/paste it, reformulate it, ask AI to generate something they didn't even read and then post.

text:
Spot on by @Chaamacoo. 🎯

People keep trying to put $SIVE / $SIVEF in a single box. It’s not just an "AI stock", a "defense stock", or a "telecom stock." It is the foundational silicon and photonics chokepoint for all of them.

Think of them as the "Intel Inside" of the next hardware supercycle. If you want to understand the true TAM, here are the actual end-products that will rely on "Sivers Inside" to function:

🧠 AI Datacenters: Next-gen 1.6T LRO pluggable transceivers (Jabil/Marvell) & Co-Packaged Optics.

 🪖 NATO Defense & SATCOM: @allspaceltd  multi-orbit terminals for Special Forces (SOF) & Navy frigates. 

🚁 Drones (UAVs): Stealth flat-panel SATCOM connectivity and FMCW LiDAR navigation. 

🗼 Next-Gen Telecom: 5G/6G massive MIMO antennas & Fixed Wireless Access (FWA). 

🚄 Infrastructure: High-speed "Track-to-Train" gigabit broadband networks. 

🚗 Autonomous Vehicles: Advanced LiDAR sensor arrays for self-driving cars. 

⌚ Wearables & Health: Biometric optical sensors (think next-gen Apple Watch & health trackers).

You aren't buying a single theme hoping it plays out. You are buying the underlying crossroads of tomorrow's technology. The sum-of-the-parts (SOTP) valuation could be huge. ⏳🔥
$SIVE $SIVEF #Semiconductors #Photonics

"richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 2439}, {"from_index": 2465, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 2499}]}, "text": "What makes a company truly compelling is not just what it is today, but how many powerful trends it could end up riding at once. That is a big part of why $SIVE / $SIVEF stands out to me. Most investors build exposure one theme at a time. They buy one company for AI, another for defense, another for satcom, another for photonics, and maybe yet another for next-generation telecom. It is a bit like assembling the future one puzzle piece at a time, hoping the picture eventually comes together. Sivers feels different. It feels more like finding one piece that already connects several of the most important parts of that picture. At first glance, it is easy to label it as just another semiconductor company. But the more I look at it, the more that feels like an oversimplification. Sivers sits in the overlap between mmWave, photonics, datacenter connectivity, satcom and defense. Those are not niche or isolated markets. They are major long-term themes, and the company has exposure to several of them at once. That is what makes the setup so compelling. Sivers does not need every single door to open at the same time for the story to work. It may be enough for one or two of these markets to accelerate, one or two partnerships to deepen, or one part of the business to gain meaningful commercial traction before investors begin to see the company in a very different light. That is why I think the “one stock, several themes” angle matters here. For a new investor, Sivers may be easier to understand if you stop thinking about it as a single narrow bet and start thinking about it as a small platform with multiple shots on goal. Not one straight road, but a crossroads. Not one engine, but several potential drivers of value that could begin contributing at different times. Of course, that does not remove the risk. Stories with several moving parts are never simple. But they can also create exactly the kind of asymmetry that makes small-cap investing so interesting, especially when the market has not fully decided how to categorize the company yet. And that is often where the upside starts to emerge. So the appeal of Sivers, to me, is not just that it has exposure to attractive end markets. It is that you do not need to buy five different companies to get that exposure. You are looking at one company that could potentially benefit from several major technology shifts at once. That kind of setup is rare. And that is why I think Sivers deserves a much closer look."}}}, "rest_id": "2045914937076183188", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "15183", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2045961814413115759", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "7487", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Photonics is a stack! 
$AXTI $AIXA $TSEM $AEHR $LITE $GLW

Here is a clean map of the sector.
Please bookmark & share! 

A laser, a photonic chip, a finished module, and a deployed AI network all sit at different points in one long chain. 

Different companies get paid for totally different reasons and on totally different timelines.

I just updated my layer framework so you can instantly see where every major name fits that I track (and why the AI buildout lifts the whole stack).

Quick map:

Layer 1 (Materials & Platforms): $AXTI $ALMU $LWLG $IQE
Layer 2 (Epitaxy Tools): $AIXA $ALRIB $VECO
Layer 3 (Foundries & Scale): $TSEM $FN $SANM
Layer 4 (Test & Yield): $AEHR $FORM $COHU $TER $VIAV
Layer 5 (Devices & Engines): $LITE $COHR $AAOI $POET $SIVE $MTSI
Layer 6 (Connectivity & Networks): $CRDO $MRVL $SMTC $CIEN $NOK $GLW

There are many more names in the industry that didn't make this list. 

Which are you tracking the closest?

text:
Agreed—the signals are aligning faster than consensus expects. Photonics holding RS while semis pause and energy narratives mature points to the transfer layer waking up. 

Group rotation in optical components + capex pivots toward interconnect efficiency (not just more FLOPs) is the tell. COHR’s positioning in that bandwidth chokepoint looks timely.

What’s your read on the next confirmation trigger—earnings language or order flow?

@grok It’s already happening @grok Semis are crowded.
Energy is understood. Now you’re seeing:

-photonics holding strength
-group rotation instead of single names
-capex shifting toward interconnect + bandwidth

That’s the early phase. Not obvious yet…but it’s forming.

text:
THE OPTICAL PHOTONICS BOTTLENECK...🧐

​As AI clusters blast past the physical limits of copper interconnects, the bottleneck is shifting hard into high-speed optics.

​These are the key players building the essential layers of the AI optical stack:

$AAOI - Transceiver Layer

Dominating with vertically integrated U.S.-based InP laser manufacturing. Already landed a major 1.6T order ($200M+) from a leading hyperscaler, plus strong 800G follow-on business.

$CRDO - Connectivity Layer

Powering high-speed links via AECs and advanced silicon. 
The DustPhotonics acquisition brings silicon photonics in-house, a massive edge as copper constraints intensify.

$LITE - Laser & Switching

Leader in EMLs and optical circuit switching. 
Backed by a $2B strategic investment from $NVDA with an optical switch backlog stretching into 2028.

$COHR - InP Infrastructure

Addressing the critical Indium Phosphide bottleneck. 
Holds a $2B $NVDA commitment and is scaling capacity aggressively for 1.6T+ networking.

$CIEN - Transport Layer

The backbone that ties everything together. 
Their solutions enable hyperscalers to move enormous AI traffic between data centers as clusters scale to gigawatt levels.

$FN - Manufacturing Backbone

The "picks-and-shovels" partner. As the reliable assembly giant for optical modules, they fuel the entire ramp where speed and scale determine the winners.

$MRVL - DSP & Electro-Optics

Strengthening the infrastructure with PAM DSPs and custom chips. 
The Celestial AI deal and NVLink exposure place them at the heart of next-gen cluster design.

$AEHR - Testing & Reliability

Providing essential wafer-level burn-in (FOX-XP). 
Recently secured a major leader in networking for 1.6T production ramp (critical for hyperscale reliability).

The transition from copper is no longer optional 😬
Anyway these stocks are volatile, with risks around customer concentration, production ramps (InP/SiPho), and high valuations. DYOR. NFA.

text:
$MRVL just made a move in the AI infrastructure race, acquiring Polariton Technologies to lock down the shift to 3.2T optical connectivity. 

​Why this is a big deal:
• ​Adds ultra-fast, low-power 
  plasmonics-based silicon photonics 
  to their stack.
• ​Solves the massive bandwidth and 
   power bottlenecks in next-gen AI 
   data centers.
• ​Positions Marvell perfectly as 
  architectures evolve beyond the 
  current 1.6T standard.

​Data centers need pure speed without melting the grid. Marvell is buying the exact tech to make it happen. 

$MRVL up 1.5% AH.

The matter is now closed and the company has regained compliance with Nasdaq regulations.

Copper Wall thesis is sharp—the shift to silicon photonics/CPO + quantum is real for AI power efficiency.

$5 each into the 12 stocks weekly = $60/wk, ~$15,600 over 5 years.

Silicon photonics market is forecast to grow 25-30% CAGR to ~$9-11B by 2030 (multiple sources). Quantum adds 20-40% upside potential.

In a strong AI adoption scenario, this basket (heavy on semis/photonics leaders like TSM/AVGO/MRVL + speculative IONQ) could compound 20-35% annualized on average—turning it into $28k-$45k by 2031 via DCA.

High volatility/risk though—some names are small-cap bets. Solid long-term play if the buildout accelerates. I'll watch updates. 🚀

text: Seeing $TSEM going Parabolic is what got me so obsessed with $GFS doing the same thing, until I started reading how GloFo's photonics aren't exactly up to par with Towers, and realized why the stock has been so badly shorted 😵 Anyway, they are starting to catch up, and $AMD is using $GFS for the their MI500 GPUs! If I'm right, GlobalFoundries will hit $100 by early November where my Finger Icon is pointing, when it intersects the 3/1 Gann Fan Angle from it's July'22 low, and the 1/1 Gann Fan Angle from it's April'25 low.   $GFS is shorted to the gills with 40% of the float short, the photonics bonanza could definitely send this sucker to $100 in frenzy, bookmarking this post in case it turns out as predicted.

user_name: ゆるっと半導体｜AI・半導体ニュースを毎朝やさしく解説
text:
光コンピュータって聞いたことある？ボクがいま注目してるのは、オックスフォード大学発祥のLumaiという企業なんだ。

ElectronicsじゃなくPhotonics（光）でAIチップの課題を解く。通常のGPUは電子で計算するけど、光は速度が違うんだよ。消費電力も少なくて済む。

実はこのアプローチ、業界内では「次の大波」って見られてるんだ。Lumaiだけじゃなく、新興企業が光演算にシフトしてるのは、NVIDIAも監視してるぐらい。

2026年いま、データセンターの電力危機が本当に深刻化してる。そこに光が答えになるかもしれないってのが、ベンチャーから大手まで考えてることなんだ。

→ 長期投資家は、TSMC製造の半導体だけじゃなく『素材から新しい技術体系』が育つ瞬間も追う価値があるよ。

text:
Let's look at the harsh realities of scaling AI clusters and why the transition to light is mandatory:
​🔴 Copper Interconnects: Overheat and lose severe signal integrity at extreme data rates (800G and 1.6T).
🟢 Silicon Photonics: Uses lasers to transfer data near the speed of light with a fraction of the power consumption.
​With hyperscalers burning billions on electricity, is moving data optically the only way to prevent a total grid collapse?
$ASML $AVGO #semiconductors #ai
(2/4)

text:
THE PHOTONICS ROTATION

Almost nobody is watching photonics.

As AI clusters scale, copper hits physical limits and the next bottleneck becomes optical infrastructure.

Here are 15 names positioned for it:

1. $LITE owns the laser + optical switching side of the trade and is one of the cleanest pure plays on AI optical demand.

2. $COHR wins from lasers, modules, and networking hardware that power hyperscale AI infrastructure and cloud expansion.

3. $AAOI is one of the best ways to play AI optical transceivers with major hyperscaler demand for 800G and 1.6T connectivity.

4. $SIVE benefits from the push toward faster semiconductor-to-optical integration as AI infrastructure scales.

5. $MRVL controls a huge part of the DSP + interconnect story with optical networking chips and high-speed connectivity.

6. $AVGO sits at the center of AI networking through switching, custom silicon, and optical interconnect demand.

7. $ANET is the Ethernet backbone moving massive AI workloads across hyperscale clusters and data centers.

8. $GLW supplies the specialty glass + fiber needed for the optical transport layer behind AI infrastructure.

9. $JBL benefits from building and scaling the actual hardware behind networking systems and optical modules.

10. $AEHR wins from burn-in + testing demand as AI ASICs and high-power optical hardware move into production.

11. $POET is focused on lower-cost optical engines designed to improve efficiency inside AI data centers.

12. $LWLG is pushing next-gen polymer photonics that could make optical communication faster and more efficient.

13. $QCLS brings exposure to advanced laser systems supporting precision photonics and next-gen optical demand.

14. $LPTH provides specialty optics and photonic components tied to industrial, defense, and AI-driven systems.

15. $ALAB gives exposure to the connectivity + infrastructure side helping AI clusters scale faster.

Most people won’t care until these are already up 100%.

text:
Three distinct approaches to quantum hardware are now trading as the sector's most active stocks by dollar volume: trapped-ion systems with major cloud integrations, quantum annealing with hybrid solvers, and integrated photonics running at room temperature.

This lineup reveals the sheer diversity of bets the market is placing. These aren't just competitors on the same track—they represent fundamentally different architectures, each with its own thesis on how quantum advantage arrives. Cloud-accessible ion traps, purpose-built optimization machines, and portable photonic devices barely share a vocabulary, let alone a roadmap.

That diversity is a sign of a maturing ecosystem. Capital spreading across competing paradigms shows the market is pricing in genuine technical uncertainty, which is the honest position to hold right now. Nobody has a monopoly on the path to broad commercial adoption.

The common thread is that all three are building real access points for enterprise users, whether through cloud marketplaces, developer toolkits, or turnkey hardware. Providers lowering the barrier to experimentation today are positioning themselves to capture demand when quantum workloads scale tomorrow #QuantumComputing

These aren't just competitors on the same track—they represent fundamentally different architectures, each with its own thesis on how quantum advantage arrives. Cloud-accessible ion traps, purpose-built optimization machines, and portable photonic devices barely share a vocabulary, let alone a roadmap. That diversity is a sign of a maturing ecosystem. is the most comprehensive photonics stack there is. L1: Materials & Substrates The foundation. Without this, nothing above it exists. L1 companies grow the raw crystal wafers – InP, GaAs, Ge that silicon can’t replace for high-speed photonics. Key names: $AXTI, $SOI, $IQE, $AIXA, $COHR, $LASR Top performer: AXT (AXTI) +6,837% InP demand exploded for AI datacenter optical connectivity. Q3 2025 InP revenues up 250%+ sequentially. Doubling capacity by end of 2026. L2: Active Photonic Devices This is where light gets made. Lasers, modulators, photodetectors. In a CPO world, these get embedded directly into switch ASICs – no longer pluggable add-ons. That’s a structural demand shift, not a cycle. Key names: $LITE, $COHR, $LASR, $SIVEF, $AAOI, $MTSI, $SMTC Top performer: Lumentum (LITE) +1,685% Named NVIDIA collaborator on Spectrum-X silicon photonics. Recently received the largest single CPO laser purchase commitment in company history. L3: Electro-Optical Connectivity ICs The translators. Silicon that bridges optical and electrical worlds. TIAs, drivers, DSPs, SerDes, retimers. As speeds push to 800G and 1.6T, this layer becomes a chokepoint. Key names: $MTSI, $SMTC, $MRVL, $CRDO, $ALAB, $MXLV Top performer: Credo (CRDO) +361% Not a pure photonics play – but every 800G and 1.6T deployment runs through their AEC and SerDes IP. L4: PIC Platform & IP The brain of the photonic system. Waveguides, modulators, and photodetectors on a single chip. Highest-IP layer in the stack. Whoever locks platform relationships here wins the next decade. Key names: $POET, $MRVL, $CRDO, $IOLI, $EOPT (Ayar Labs, Lightmatte) Top performer: Innolight +948% Partnered with Tower Semi on a breakthrough SiPh platform that cuts lasers per module in half. L5: Foundry & Process Platform You need a fab. Not just any fab. SiPh fabrication needs specialized processes – TFLN, SiPh COUPE, SoIC – that only a handful of foundries can run globally. Key names: $TSEM, $GFS, $UMC, $TSM Top performer: Tower Semi (TSEM) +542% SiPh revenue hit ~$52M in Q3 2025 alone, up 70% YoY. Full-year 2025 target: $220M+. Q4 annualized run rate: $320M+. Investing $650M to triple SiPh shipments by mid-2026. L6A: Engine & Module Where the optical engine becomes a product – 400G, 800G, 1.6T modules. Most commoditized layer today. CPO transition will redraw it entirely. Key names: $COHR, $LITE, $AAOI, $FN, $POET, $MRVL, $CRDO Top performer: Lumentum (LITE) +1,685% Vertically integrated across L2 and L6A. Supply locked by hyperscalers. Pricing power persists. L6B: Infrastructure Fiber and cable. The physical nervous system of every AI cluster. Boring until hyperscalers start spending $50B+ on datacenter campuses. Then it’s anything but. Key names: $GLW, $OTCPK:SMOKY, $PRY, $APH, $FJKUF Top performer: Fujikura (5803.TSE) +748% Japanese fiber giant. Direct beneficiary of AI intra-datacenter cabling demand going parabolic. L6C: Transport Moves data between datacenters across metro and long-haul fiber. As AI clusters get larger and more distributed, inter-datacenter bandwidth demand compounds. This layer doesn’t slow down. Key names: $CIEN, $NOK, $CSCO Top performer: Ciena (CIEN) +777% WaveLogic coherent optics is the dominant long-haul platform in North America and Europe L7: Test & Qualification The final gatekeeper. Every photonic device passes through here before shipping. Burn-in, optical test, IC test, wafer test. Often overlooked. Often the highest-margin layer. text:
$LPK $LPKF 🔥
This company is not only successful in the laser and photonics sector, but also active in the solar and quantum computing space, where it collaborates with companies such as Volkswagen AG and Infineon Technologies AG.

The market cap of this company appears extremely undervalued, especially considering that its patented LIDE technology for glass substrates has massive game-changing potential.

In my view, the current valuation doesn’t even come close to reflecting its true potential.

text:
Interesting take on the interconnect bottleneck. Photonics is indeed the physics play for scaling beyond copper limits—lower latency, power per bit, and heat as racks densify for AI training/inference. 

Data movement at exascale is the next constraint after raw FLOPs. COHR's optical tech sits right in that transfer layer. Capital cycles rotate to the real chokepoints. What's your edge on timing the shift?

Because copper-based systems hit limits:

-Heat
-Signal loss
-Power inefficiency
 
Photonics solves that. It allows data centers to scale without melting down. That’s where $COHR sits.
Right inside the transfer layer.

This is physics. You can’t scale AI if data can’t move efficiently.

text:
As models scale, something breaks:

-Bandwidth
-Latency
-Power per bit

That’s the real constraint forming. 
This is where photonics matters.

Light moves data faster
-More efficiently
-With less heat

That’s the shift most people haven’t priced in yet:
electrical → optical

Fascinated with the names associated with the 800V DC Architecture (Power Conversion / Power Semi's Theme)!

Neo-clouds cooking with hot grease recently, but look a bit over extended. Nebius smartly consolidating. 

CPU's and broader semi packaging names look unbelievably strong, but a bit extended here.

Space trade is hot, but needs some sideways action.

text:
With a background in atomic precision measurements, I'm more optics, quantum sensing, and photonics "focused" in my current role, obviously for other applications, but thought if there was an intersection somewhere, some of the signatures of the Chronoflux could be probed with the sensors under development.

I haven't worked on Casimir devices yet, but they are on my radar. I am interested in phononic photonic resonators, especially in relation to tests of Orch OR, as well as PIC frequency combs for other sensing applications. So there's definitely some overlap.

Would be nice if you could implement the equations function in this document 🙏

The two Substack articles profile Nynomic AG as a German photonics holding company with LayTec as its key subsidiary, a quasi-monopolist in in-situ metrology for MOCVD reactors and etch processes powering AI data center lasers (InP) and power electronics (GaN). They highlight restructuring savings lifting 2026 margins to 6-8%, vertical integration across the value chain, and massive TAM expansion from optical transceivers and co-packaged optics. My analysis sees strong moats from proprietary databases, OEM lock-ins with Aixtron (90%+ share), and recurring software upsells despite past guidance misses and cyclical risks. Growth potential is substantial at 3-5x for LayTec revenue by 2028 driven by AI buildout, with Nynomic re-rating to peer multiples implying 2-3x stock upside. This stems from validated photonics bottlenecks via NVIDIA investments and staggered order cycles already showing Q4 2025 inflection.

text:
$GFS GlobalFoundries Inc. (GFS) Q4 FY2025 Post-Earnings Debrief

https://t.co/vZ2dYs7a7E

Thesis: GlobalFoundries delivered a Q4 FY2025 print that pushes the underwriting debate away from a mature-node recovery and toward whether silicon photonics, optical-module and advanced-packaging capabilities, CID growth, and geographically resilient specialty capacity can support a structurally higher-growth and higher-margin model. The strongest proof that this shift is already happening is that 2025 was the first full year with more than 60% of revenue outside smart mobile devices and automotive plus CID reached a record one-third of revenue, while elevated FY2026 capex and lower FCF conversion remain the central execution risk.

-Q1 2026 revenue, gross margin, and diluted EPS versus the $1.625B / 27% / $0.35 guide framework
-Investor Day detail on the silicon photonics bridge, including pluggables versus CPO timing, module-yield milestones, and the 40% gross-margin path
-Whether silicon photonics remains overwhelmingly pluggables-driven in 2026 while keeping the end-2028 $1B run-rate target credible
-Whether Malta optical-module flow, advanced packaging, and 200mm/300mm dual-region capacity support faster revenue conversion without yield disruption
-Whether the structural mix shift, now above 60% non-mobile revenue and one-third auto plus CID, keeps widening in 2026
-Whether government-support offsets, customer prepayments, and corridor-level debottlenecking keep FY2026 net capex and FCF conversion within the guided algorithm

PHOTONICS BOTTLENECK As AI clusters scale past copper’s physical limits, the bottleneck shifts to optical & these are the companies building that layer across the stack: 1. $AAOI building the transceiver layer of the AI network through vertically integrated U.S.-based InP laser manufacturing. It has already secured over $200M in its first volume 1.6T order from one hyperscale customer followed by another $124M in 800G orders from a second. 2. $AEHR building the reliability layer for the optical & AI hardware stack through burn-in & test systems. It just received a record $41M follow-on order from its lead hyperscale customer reinforcing the idea that Sonoma is becoming a key production burn-in platform for high-power AI ASICs. 3. $CRDO building the connectivity layer that helps AI clusters move data faster through active electrical cables, retimers & high-speed interconnect silicon. The DustPhotonics acquisition also extends that platform into silicon photonics before copper becomes a real constraint. 4. $LITE building the laser layer of the AI optical stack through EMLs, optical components & optical switching exposure. The setup is backed by a $2B $NVDA strategic investment & optical circuit switch backlog above $400M with orders reportedly extending through 2028. 5. $VIAV building the testing & validation layer of the optical stack through network instrumentation & photonics measurement tools. It is the picks-and-shovels layer of the transition because every high-speed optical buildout still needs to be tested regardless of which transceiver vendor wins. 6. $COHR building one of the core photonics bottlenecks through indium phosphide lasers, optical engines & communications components tied to next-gen AI networking. It also has a $2B $NVDA strategic investment behind it & is doubling InP device capacity into the 1.6T ramp. 7. $MRVL building the DSP & optical infrastructure layer through electro-optics, PAM DSPs, interconnect silicon & custom networking chips. The Celestial AI deal & NVLink Fusion exposure both strengthen its position as photonics becomes more central to AI cluster design."}}}, "rest_id": "2045885512922419251", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "409051", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2047051196981223802", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "153735", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Marvell $MRVL just announced it has agreed to acquire Polariton Technologies, 

"a developer of high-speed, low-power plasmonics-based silicon photonics devices"

Terms of the deal were not disclosed

$MRVL ... Marvell to acquire Polariton Technologies to advance next-gen silicon photonics and optical interconnects; financial terms not disclosed (157.32+6.01)
MRVL announced the acquisition of Polariton Technologies, a developer of high-speed, low-power plasmonics-based silicon photonics devices. The acquisition strengthens Marvell's optical technology portfolio by adding advanced modulation capabilities that enable continued scaling in bandwidth, power efficiency, and integration for next-generation coherent and optical interconnect platforms.

text:
It's highly nuanced, and I'll explain why it's not late, but late to some: 

Photonics is the newest supercycle (maybe H1 into H2 2025 was the start).

Then there's many different architectural changes in each supercycle:

-> $LITE, $COHR, Innolight, $AXTI and these names led the first 

I did a thesis post on mentioning all four of them as the largest beneficiaries (all are up 500-1000% 1Y)

-> $AAOI, $JBL and others types of names are benefit immensely as the transitional bridge (eg. 1.6T pluggable) 

-> $SIVE, Celestial, Ayar, $POET are others future gens eg. CPO (what I'm focusing on now)

-> VisEra, QD Laser, $ALMU and others are likely going to be future gens (quantum dot, different packaging types, etc) if you fast forward 4 years. 

Of course, $LITE does everything. $AXTI will be used for everything. 

But the amount of pure play exposure for each architectural shift in each mini supercycle is different. 

For example, inp usage with quantum dot is still there, but less used. Or DFB laser arrays for CPO instead of EML. 

There's probably still 50%+ with $LITE and $COHR. And you're a little on the "late" side of things. 

But you're extremely early to new architecture generations. 

What I'm trying to do is point regular retail investors into the direction of new gold mines for free. 

Before institutions figure out sooner or later by paying $20k for equity research reports.

text:
Copper Wall Account Update 🚀

Launched this account last Monday (4/20) — already +4% in under a week.
$5/week DCA into my AI/photonics basket:

$MRVL (data center semis & networking)
$AVGO (AI networking & semiconductors)
$FN (optical component manufacturing)
$CRDO (high-speed connectivity for AI)
$TSEM (specialty analog foundry)
$ARM (CPU architecture powering AI chips)
$ALAB (AI/cloud connectivity solutions)
$TSM (world’s leading chip foundry)
$LITE (optical & photonic components)
$AAOI (fiber-optic networking products)
$HON (industrial tech & automation)
$IONQ (trapped-ion quantum computing)

Thesis: Photonics could be the next major unlock in data processing, and we’re only in Year 3 of a 10-year AI buildout.

This account should look strong by 2032. 

Bullish? 💡📈

photonique, elle fabrique des atténuateurs Optiques Variables (VOA) MEMSCAP, à la base, c’est surtout un fournisseur de capteurs de pression de très haute précision pour l’aéronautique et le médical. Mais ils ont aussi un segment communications optiques. Ils fournissent des VOA basés sur technologie MEMS, utilisés pour gérer dynamiquement la puissance des signaux dans les réseaux fibre optique (datacenters, télécoms, amplis EDFA). Sur ce segment de niche, MEMSCAP est tout simplement leader mondial des puces MEMS VOA, avec une part de marché estimée à plus de 80% sur un marché d’environ 5 M$ au niveau puces, et 50 M$ au niveau produits finis. Pas l'ASML de la photonique mais une boite qui pourrait se rerater rapidement. D'ailleurs hier il y a eu un afflux plutôt anormal, ce qui me fait potentiellement penser à un positionnement d’un bon gros player. Si les microcaps continuent d'exploser, je parie que Memscap en bénéficiera probablement."}}}, "rest_id": "2045528106413199710", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "347226", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2045914495139099056", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "775", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

Here's a quick summary of the 7 stocks building the optical photonics layer for AI clusters (as copper limits hit):

1. **$AAOI** – Transceivers & U.S. InP lasers (already $324M+ orders)
2. **$AEHR** – Burn-in/test systems for high-power AI chips ($41M record order)
3. **$CRDO** – High-speed cables, retimers & silicon photonics
4. **$LITE** – Lasers, components & optical switches ($2B NVDA backing)
5. **$VIAV** – Testing & measurement tools (picks-and-shovels play)
6. **$COHR** – InP lasers & optical engines ($2B NVDA backing)
7. **$MRVL** – DSPs, interconnect silicon & custom AI networking chips

These target the optical bottleneck as AI scales. (Logos match the post image.)

text:
$GFS — GlobalFoundries: The Only “China-Free” Silicon Photonics Foundry at Scale
Everyone needs a fab. GlobalFoundries acquired Singapore’s Advanced Micro Foundry (AMF) — arguably the most important pure-play silicon photonics fab outside the U.S. — giving GlobalFoundries a claim to being the largest dedicated SiPh foundry in the world. Its “GF Fotonix” platform has become the go-to for fabless photonics companies, and the AMF acquisition allows it to offer a China-free supply chain for optical chips — a selling point that may become mandatory for U.S. hyperscalers.  In a world of supply chain paranoia, this is a structural moat hiding in plain sight.

text:
$MKSI — MKS Instruments: The Lab Equipment That Makes Photonics Manufacturing Possible
Nobody builds photonics fabs without MKS. MKS spotlighted new photonics solutions for AI, quantum, and biophotonics applications at Photonics West 2026, drawing fresh analyst upgrades.  With projected earnings growth of 24% in 2026, MKSI has the potential to continue its rally.  Laser power meters, beam delivery, optical process control — the unsexy tools that make every photonics fab run. If the fab count doubles, MKSI equipment demand doubles with it.

text:
$CRDO — Credo Semiconductor: Just Bought Its Way Into Silicon Photonics With a $750M Power Move
Already on the list as a small cap — but this news changes the thesis entirely. Credo agreed to acquire DustPhotonics for $750M, bringing industry-leading Silicon Photonics PIC technology in-house and expanding Credo’s addressable market across 800G, 1.6T, and 3.2T optical transceivers — positioning it with a vertically integrated connectivity stack spanning SerDes, DSP, Silicon Photonics, and system integration.  Management targets combined optical revenue above $500M by fiscal 2027.  Credo went from a DSP play to a full photonics stack company in one deal. Re-rate incoming.

text:
$IQEPF — IQE PLC: The UK Epitaxial Wafer Supplier That 10x’d in Four Months
IQE PLC, a struggling UK semiconductor firm with a market value of less than $70M at the start of 2026, soared more than 10 times in just four months  as photonics demand re-rated the entire substrate layer. IQE is one of Lumentum’s key suppliers for Indium Phosphide (InP) — the material used to make high-performance data center lasers that silicon cannot generate on its own.  Highest volatility on this list. Trades OTC in the U.S. as $IQEPF. If you want the rawest exposure to the InP chokepoint, this is it — with all the execution risk that comes with a company undergoing a strategic review.

Traditional optical component makers like Coherent have pivoted from transceiver assembly to becoming laser foundries — critical component manufacturers rather than finished goods producers. A structurally better position.

text: $GLW — Corning: The Fiber Glass Monopoly That Runs Under Every Data Center. Corning is the dominant fiber glass supplier — what the photonics supply chain calls a necessary materials company. Durable, defensible, boring in the best way.

"Tm90ZVR3ZWV0OjIwNDU2MjYyNjA5MzI2ODk5MjA=", "media": {"inline_media": []}, "richtext": {"richtext_tags": []}, "text": "$VIAV: A Crucial Role In The AI Optical Supply Chain Let’s start with a thought You can fab all the lasers, build all the transceivers, lay all the fiber, and rack all the switches, but none of it goes live until someone proves the entire network actually works under real AI workloads. That last-mile validation layer is where $VIAV sits. And as clusters get denser and speeds jump from 800G → 1.6T → 3.2T, this is becoming a more crucial role The question we need to answer is whether or not the whole fabric behaves, scales, and stays resilient under live AI traffic. $VIAV owns the tools that sit right after the optical hardware and right before full network confidence like: lab/production test platforms ultra-dense fiber certification gear system-level validation. Key products already hitting hyperscalers: 1) TestCenter D2 1.6T Appliance - generates real AI workloads and validates performance, scale, and multi-vendor interoperability at 1.6T. Management says current validation tech is “struggling to keep pace with AI.” 2) DCX 700 optical loss test set - certifies up to 24 fibers at once in dense data-center plants. Data-center demand has jumped to about a third of field instruments. 3) Hollow-core fiber tester - already in trials with three leading hyperscalers for campus-scale AI interconnects. Lower latency, longer reach. Management says fiber value chain “firing at full speed,” with hyperscalers demanding tighter control over every strand. Numbers are catching up: - Q2 revenue $369M (+36% y/y) - NSE (the AI/data-center engine) +46% and now 79% of total. - Business mix flipped: only ~40% traditional telco, 60% data-center + A&D. Q3 guide shows momentum continuing through calendar 2026. New Spirent assets tracking ~$200M run rate. Restructuring is ongoing, cutting slow segments to double down on data center and defense. Keep this one on your radar!
